danger_signals.visual_form: Character stance changes and battle positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat engagement - opponent ready to fight

danger_signals.visual_form: Character transformation animations and special move effects
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage threat imminent - character entering powered state
Increasing danger to player character from accumulated damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Character idle poses and neutral positioning
safety_signals.meaning: No immediate threat - preparation or victory state

safety_signals.visual_form: Character selection screen and match bracketing
safety_signals.meaning: Structured competition environment - established rules in effect

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Clan members distinguished by individual character designs and fighting styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament setting as clan competition arena

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Foreign character archetypes (Italian strongwoman, English aristocrat, American playboy)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Non-clan members with distinct cultural visual identities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Characters from outside Japan competing in clan tournament

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament bracket structure; elderly clan head (Oume Gōketsuji) as final boss; multiple family members as playable competitors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters motivated to fight for leadership position; each character has individual story stakes related to clan hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character relationships depicted through story narratives; Otane vs Oume dynamic as central tension
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sibling rivalry manifested through combat; abuse survivor narrative drives Otane's tournament participation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: International character roster with distinct visual designs reflecting national origins
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters from different nations brought into Japanese family tournament context

environmental_storytelling.example: Pre-fight character biographies pop up between demos (in Japanese version), establishing narrative context for each fighter's motivation
Each character has specific win quotes for defeated foes, creating narrative relationships and hierarchies through post-battle dialogue
Visual appearance directly communicates nationality and social class (Italian strongwoman Angela Belti, English aristocrat Annie Hamilton, American playboy Keith Wayne)
Gōketsuji family members visually identifiable within larger character roster, establishing faction membership through design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Functions as final boss; unplayable in base game (palette-swap character initially); represents authority and power to be challenged
Playable tournament participant; driven by motivation to overcome elder sister and prove worthy of leadership
Execute double jumps, run attacks, and character transformations; transformation feature shows characters changing appearance and fighting style mid-combat
Participate in internal clan tournament despite being outsiders; motivations tied to personal romance (Keith Wayne) and adventure rather than clan succession

transformation_system.visual_communication: Several characters can change appearance and fighting style by executing specific moves (usually throwing moves)
transformation_system.meaning: State change in combat readiness and strategy; visual transformation indicates power escalation or tactical shift
transformation_system.world_state_reflection: Demonstrates superhuman nature of clan members; multiple personas reflect absurdist humor tone

absurdist_visual_language.technique: Visual comedy through character design and narrative incongruity
absurdist_visual_language.examples: Strongwoman Angela Belti's personality conflicts with appearance; English aristocrat Annie Hamilton with unusual pet collection; tournament premise as vehicle for absurd character interactions
absurdist_visual_language.world_state_meaning: Softens danger signals through humor; makes serious succession conflict palatable through comedic character archetypes

notes: Power Instinct 2 uses character design, narrative framing, and transformation mechanics to communicate a world state of clan succession crisis. Visual signals prioritize character distinction and absurdist personality over traditional danger/safety cues. The game communicates political situation (leadership vacuum) through tournament structure and character motivations rather than environmental aesthetics. Faction identity relies heavily on character archetype and visual design rather than color coding or environmental markers. Transformation system serves dual purpose: practical gameplay mechanic and visual manifestation of superhuman clan member abilities.
